text.title: resource-management Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for resource-management as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: 100% allocation plans that ignore meetings, email, ceremonies, and breaks.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: These plans always slip on the first sick week or holiday; they create chronic delivery failures that look like execution problems but are actually planning failures.

failure-mode.description: Calendar API pulls miss informal meetings, pair sessions, and debugging deep dives.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Structured calendar data only captures formally scheduled events; ask the person to confirm their actual available hours before using the raw calendar count for planning.

failure-mode.description: Allocation agent packs the calendar to 100% if uncapped.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents optimize for filling capacity; hard-cap at 80% effective in the prompt and the output schema to prevent burnout plans.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Mid-sprint reassignments break WIP and disrupt flow. Schedule rebalancing only at sprint boundaries.

failure-mode.description: Agents read calendar subjects to assess meeting load.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Calendar subjects are often sensitive (layoff prep, performance review); redact subject lines before any LLM input, keep only duration and recurrence pattern.
